scores.justification: Machine-checked method with honestly scoped modeling decisions; the definitional character of the headline collapse remains confronted in Remark 4.6; v10 diff is prose/bibliography only, leaving the audited proof base unchanged.
80 kernel-verified results, 0 sorries, 1 inert axiom; per-file line/declaration arithmetic exact and unchanged from the v9-audited artifact; no Lean file touched in v10.

Central claim displayed as a one-sentence quote in the introduction; eight numbered one-sentence contributions with section pointers; the compressed ~262-word abstract still names every headline result.

v9's deduction closed: Wehmeier 2004 and Rogers & Wehmeier 2012 (RSL) now engaged substantively and accurately in Section 5 with a contact sentence in Section 4.6, on an honest complementarity framing with correct bibliographic details; remaining prior work engaged on its merits.

Pinned toolchain, public repo, CI, step-by-step build appendix; this review re-ran the 3-pass pdflatex recipe from scratch and content-matched the committed PDF; Lean artifact facts verified on this master lineage.
Both TikZ figures self-contained at caption level, black-only per venue with pattern-based encodings; appendix tables have meaningful headers and booktabs alignment; untouched in v10.

scores.fix: Replace 'Neither work treats identity by the exclusive convention' with 'Our formalization does not treat identity by the exclusive convention'; also fix the Section 4.3 'three new results' leftover.
scores.justification: Flow intact, hedging calibrated, render pass clean (0 overfull, 0 broken refs), but the new Wehmeier paragraph ends with a mis-drafted sentence that is false on its natural reading - the same paragraph describes those works' exclusive identity treatment two sentences earlier; the intended subject is this paper's own development. Carried-over v9 nit ('The three new results') also remains.

23/23 cites resolve with no orphans; the two new entries (rogers2012, wehmeier2004) are complete with DOIs and Crossref-verified; prose claims about sources accurate where checkable; references/wehmeier.md claim-support note added.

v9's deduction closed: abstract compressed ~450 to ~262 words with no headline claim lost, and the conclusion isolates two consequences instead of re-running the result sequence; residual duplicated complementarity framing (Sections 4.6 and 5) is a nit below scoring threshold.

findings.rationale: 'Neither work treats identity by the exclusive convention, so their identity results have no direct analogue here' is false or self-contradictory on its natural readings: the same paragraph states that Wehmeier 2004 and Rogers & Wehmeier 2012 treat identity precisely by the exclusive convention. The intended subject is this paper's own development, which has no identity apparatus. A referee at the target venue - plausibly Wehmeier - will catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with 'Our formalization does not treat identity by the exclusive convention, so their identity results have no direct analogue here.'

findings.evidence_span: Section 4.6 contact paragraph / Section 5 Wehmeier paragraph
findings.rationale: The they-prove-completeness-on-paper vs we-mechanize-semantic-reach contrast is stated twice in similar words.
findings.suggested_fix: Trim the Section 4.6 statement to one clause plus a pointer to Section 5.

findings.rationale: Carried over from v9, unaddressed: scherf2025, an unpublished manuscript with a deleted repository (web.archive snapshot only), is still framed as 'the most directly comparable work'.
findings.suggested_fix: Demote the framing or add a published comparison point.

findings.rationale: Carried over from v9, unaddressed: 'The three new results' reads as a version-diff leftover; a fresh reader has no baseline for 'new'.
findings.suggested_fix: Say 'The three results of this subsection'.

findings.rationale: Carried over from v9, unaddressed: prose credits Fogelin with initiating the debate; the critique first appeared in the 1976 first edition, while the entry cites the 1987 second edition.
findings.suggested_fix: Cite as Fogelin (1976/1987) at first prose mention.
